assertions.description: All components are accounted for: P1-P4 (Arduino-standard connectors), J1-J10 (PropShield-specific headers), D3 and D4 (D_Schottky). Component types are correctly classified.

D3 and D4 are D_Schottky diodes. D3 has its cathode on +5V and anode on an intermediate net; D4 has its cathode on that same net and anode feeding a board output. This is a Schottky diode OR / power-mux topology. The analyzer reports protection_devices=[] and no other classification. While this is not a classic reverse-polarity protector, it is a meaningful discrete power circuit worth noting. The false_negative category applies because the circuit has recognizable structure that goes undetected.
